coverage.note: Branch gap is 100% CEILING (NestJS decorators + class-validator metadata). Functional logic coverage: service 95.81%, controller endpoints 96.59%, processor process() 100%.

findings.description: SMS message body stored in plaintext DB — PII not encrypted at rest. GDPR Art.32 violation and CLAUDE.md PII rule violation.
findings.remediation: Injected EncryptionService (AES-256-CBC). encrypt() on write (sendMessage, receiveInbound), decrypt() on read (getMessages, idempotency return).

findings.description: sendMessage() writes to DB only — no Twilio API call. Outbound SMS never actually sent to customer's phone.
findings.remediation: Inject TwilioClient (or a TwilioService wrapper), call messages.create() after DB insert. Add twilioSid to created message record.

findings.description: InboundSmsDto.tenantId accepted from untrusted Twilio webhook payload. A malicious actor who knows a valid twilioSid can spoof tenantId and inject messages into another tenant's thread.
findings.remediation: Remove tenantId from InboundSmsDto. Derive tenantId server-side: phoneHash → smsThread lookup → thread.tenantId. The phoneHash already uniquely identifies the thread+tenant.

findings.description: SmsThreadController (JWT-protected) has no @Throttle() decorator. Authenticated endpoints GET /threads, GET /threads/:id/messages, POST /threads/:id/send are not rate-limited.
findings.remediation: Add @Throttle({ default: { ttl: 60000, limit: 100 } }) at class level on SmsThreadController.

findings.description: getOrCreateThread has theoretical TOCTOU race: two parallel requests could both read null and both attempt create. Mitigated by DB unique constraint [tenantId, customerId, phoneHash] and BullMQ serial processing for inbound.
findings.remediation: ACCEPTED_PATTERN — DB constraint prevents actual data corruption. Advisory lock not warranted given BullMQ serialization.

findings.description: HMAC URL built from req.protocol + req.get('host'). If reverse proxy does not forward X-Forwarded-Proto, protocol may be 'http' while Twilio signed 'https', causing HMAC mismatch and false webhook rejections.
findings.remediation: Document: NestJS app must set app.set('trust proxy', 1) or equivalent. Add runtime check with warn if NODE_ENV=production and X-Forwarded-Proto not present.

findings.description: HMAC-SHA1 used for Twilio webhook signature verification. SHA1 is deprecated per NIST SP 800-131A Rev.2 (2019). Twilio's new webhook security API supports SHA256.
findings.remediation: Migrate to HMAC-SHA256 using Twilio Webhook Validator v2 (requires opt-in on Twilio console). Low urgency: SHA1 not broken for HMAC-specific use case (collision attacks don't apply to HMAC mode).

findings.description: Logger.log includes tenantId in message string. Reduces log security posture — tenantId is internal identifier that should not appear in application logs.
findings.remediation: Remove tenantId from log message. Use structured logger context or omit entirely: this.logger.log(`Processed job ${job.id}`).

ceilingsAccepted.reason: NestJS @Injectable() multi-parameter constructor generates Istanbul BRDA entries via __decorate/__metadata. Not coverable in unit tests.
NestJS multi-decorator class (@ApiTags, @ApiBearerAuth, @UseGuards, @Roles, @Controller) and @InjectQueue constructor generate Istanbul BRDA entries.
@Processor() + extends WorkerHost + super() call in constructor generate Istanbul BRDA entries. BullMQ WorkerHost class hierarchy not coverable in NestJS unit test module.
class-validator decorators (@IsString, @IsOptional, @MaxLength, @IsUUID) generate decorator metadata BRDA entries. DTO decorator branches cannot be covered in unit tests.

sources: WebSearch: NestJS SMS best practices performance patterns 2026
